mnemonics: LITERALS are the LITERAL (actual) values you literally type into your code.
keyPoints: Literals are fixed values directly represented in the source code, like numbers, characters, or text.
Java has several types of literals: Integer, Floating-Point, Character, String, Boolean, and Null.
The type of a literal is inferred by the compiler based on its form and context (e.g., 10 is an int, 10.0 is a double).
commonMistakes: Forgetting that integer literals are of type int by default, causing errors when assigning a large number to a long (use the 'L' suffix: e.g., 12345678901L).
Using double quotes for character literals (e.g., "A") instead of single quotes (e.g., 'A'), which creates a String instead of a char.
